description:
The crate-level doc (lib.rs line 39-41) says 'Each try_sign call runs the underlying async SigningBackend::sign on a scoped thread.' The per-method doc on all three adapters repeats 'Each call creates a scoped thread with a current-thread tokio runtime.'

This is false when called outside an existing tokio runtime. In that path (sign_sync lines 64-72), the async work runs directly on the calling thread via a new current-thread runtime — no scoped thread is spawned. A scoped thread is only spawned when called from within an existing runtime.

Callers who read the docs and rely on the 'dedicated thread' guarantee for stack isolation or reentrancy will be surprised. The accurate description is: 'When called from within an existing tokio runtime, a scoped thread is spawned. Otherwise the async work runs on the calling thread.'

Locations: lib.rs lines 39-41 (crate doc), line 141-142 (Ed25519Signer try_sign), line 199-200 (EcdsaP256Signer try_sign), line 265-266 (RsaPssSigner try_sign).
title: try_sign docs falsely claim a scoped thread is always spawned
